audioscript.text: Section 1.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You'll hear a student talking to the accommodation coordinator at her school.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: First, you have some time to look at questions 1 to 4.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You'll see that there is an example which has been done for you.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: On this occasion only, the conversation relating to this will be played first.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Sara, I've heard that you want to move into a homestay family.
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: Is that correct?
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: Yes, that's right.
audioscript.speaker: SARA

audioscript.text: I've been staying with my aunt, and now my cousin is arriving from Singapore.
audioscript.speaker: SARA

audioscript.text: And my aunt needs the room for him.
audioscript.speaker: SARA

audioscript.text: Oh, that's bad luck.
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: Well I'll need to get some particulars first.
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: Um, Sara, what's your full name?
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: Sara Lim.
audioscript.speaker: SARA

audioscript.text: And that's Sara without the 'h' at the end.
audioscript.speaker: SARA

audioscript.text: Linda asks for Sara's full name, Sara Lim.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: So this has been written in the notes.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now we shall begin.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You should answer the questions as you listen, because you will not hear the recording a second time.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Listen carefully and answer questions 1 to 4.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Sara, I've heard that you want to move into a homestay family.
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: Is that correct?
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: Yes, that's right.
audioscript.speaker: SARA

audioscript.text: I've been staying with my aunt, and now my cousin is arriving from Singapore.
audioscript.speaker: SARA

audioscript.text: And my aunt needs the room for him.
audioscript.speaker: SARA

audioscript.text: Oh, that's bad luck.
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: Well, I'll need to get some particulars first.
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: Um, Sara, what's your full name?
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: Sara Lim.
audioscript.speaker: SARA

audioscript.text: And that's Sara without the 'h' at the end.
audioscript.speaker: SARA

audioscript.text: Hmm. How old are you Sara?
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: 23 only just.
audioscript.speaker: SARA

audioscript.text: It was my birthday on the 21st of August.
audioscript.speaker: SARA

audioscript.text: Ah. Happy birthday for yesterday.
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: How long have you been in Australia?
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: A year in Adelaide and 6 months in Sydney.
audioscript.speaker: SARA

audioscript.text: I prefer Sydney.
audioscript.speaker: SARA

audioscript.text: I've got more friends here.
audioscript.speaker: SARA

audioscript.text: What's your address at your aunt's house?
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: Uh. Flat 1539, Forest Road, Canterbury.
audioscript.speaker: SARA

audioscript.text: And the post code is 2 0 3 6.
audioscript.speaker: SARA

audioscript.text: OK, what are you studying now?
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: I was studying general English in Adelaide, and now I'm doing academic English, because I'm trying to get into medicine next year.
audioscript.speaker: SARA

audioscript.text: That sounds good, but it'll take you a long time.
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: When would you like to move out from your aunt's?
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: My cousin arrives on Friday morning, so I'd better be out on Thursday.
audioscript.speaker: SARA

audioscript.text: What, the seventh of September?
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: Yes, that's right.
audioscript.speaker: SARA

audioscript.text: Before you hear the rest of the conversation, you have some time to look at questions 5 to 10.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen and answer questions 5 to 10.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: That doesn't leave us much time. Right.
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: OK. I need to know what kind of accommodation you'd like, so I can get you something suitable.
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: Can I share a room with someone else?
audioscript.speaker: SARA

audioscript.text: I've been alone in my room at my aunt's, and I've always shared with my sister, and I like that.
audioscript.speaker: SARA

audioscript.text: Yes, fine.
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: That'll save you money too.
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: Would you like to live with a family, or do you think that a single person would be better for you?
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: I have lots of very nice single people on my books.
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: Do you have any women living alone, retired women?
audioscript.speaker: SARA

audioscript.text: Yes, I have quite a few whose children have grown up and left home.
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: In fact, I have some really lovely retired ladies living by themselves.
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: Who just love the company of students.
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: Most of them live in flats, but that's not a problem for you, is it?
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: Not at all.
audioscript.speaker: SARA

audioscript.text: I'm used to that.
audioscript.speaker: SARA

audioscript.text: My aunt lives in a flat too, remember?
audioscript.speaker: SARA

audioscript.text: Hmm.
audioscript.speaker: SARA

audioscript.text: I'm not used to a big house with a garden, swimming pool, pets and all that.
audioscript.speaker: SARA

audioscript.text: OK, fine.
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: I know quite a bit about what you want now.
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: I should let you know that your rent will be $160 per week.
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: You'll have to pay me $320 as a deposit.
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: Before you move in, the deposit is as insurance, in case you break something.
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: You'll need to pay monthly to me by cash or cheque, I don't mind.
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: You don't need to pay for gas, electricity or water, but you will need to pay your proportion of the phone bill.
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: Most families do that on an honor system.
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: But you'll have to wait and see.
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: Hmm. Have you got any more questions for me?
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: Uh. When will you know where I can go?
audioscript.speaker: SARA

audioscript.text: I'll work on it now.
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: So, come and see me tomorrow, and I should have some news for you then.
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: Thanks a lot.
audioscript.speaker: SARA

audioscript.text: Goodbye.
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: See you tomorrow, after lunch would be better for me.
audioscript.speaker: LYNDA

audioscript.text: OK.
audioscript.speaker: SARA

audioscript.text: See you then.
audioscript.speaker: SARA

audioscript.text: Bye.
audioscript.speaker: SARA

audioscript.text: That is the end of section 1.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers. Now turn to section 2.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

test_text.title: Summer Festival Highlights: Theatrical and Outdoor Shows

audioscript.text: Section 2, you'll hear Jane Hamilton talking about this year's summer festival.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You have some time to look at questions 11 to 14.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 11 to 14.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Good evening, and in this week's edition of 'Focus on the Arts', Jane Hemmington is going to fill us in on what's in store for us at this year's Summer Festival.
audioscript.speaker: GEOFFREY

audioscript.text: Over to you, Jane.
audioscript.speaker: GEOFFREY

audioscript.text: Thank you, Geoffrey.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: Uh. This year the summer festival is the biggest we've ever seen, so there should be something for everybody.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: This is the third year they've run it.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: And the timing slightly different, uh, for the last couple of years, it's been around the 5th to 17th, but this year, they wanted to allow everyone enough time to recover from the 1st of January celebrations.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: And they've put it at the end of the month.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: The program has sensational theater, dance, and also a large number of art exhibitions.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: But the thing the festival is most famous for is its great street music.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: For today's report, though, Geoffrey, I'm looking at some of the theatrical events that you might like to see, in particular at this year's theme, circuses.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: I'm going to tell you about two circus performances, but there are plenty of others in the program.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: I've chosen these because they represent distinct movements within circus performance.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: The first is the Circus Romano from Italy.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: As this is a traveling circus, it follows a long tradition by performing in a marquee, which is really like a canvas portable building.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: Usually put up in a green space or car park, rather than in a theater or stadium.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: Before you hear the rest of the talk, you have some time to look at questions 15 to 20.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen and answer questions 15 to 20.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: In spite of this, Circus Romano isn't at all like the traditional circuses I grew up with.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: There are no animals, just very talented clowning and acrobatic routines.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: The show has a lot of very funny moments, especially at the beginning.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: But the best part is the music and lighting, they're magical.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: At $45 it's very expensive anyway.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: Uh. It's really for adult tastes.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: In fact, much of it would be wasted on children.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: So, I suggest you leave them at home.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: The second circus performance is Circus Electrica at the Studio Theater.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: The purists are suggesting that this isn't a circus at all.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: It's a showcase for skills in dance and magic.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: Rather than the usual ones you expect in a circus, with only six performers, it's a small production which suits the venue well.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: The studio only seats about 200 people.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: For my money, it's the aerial displays which are outstanding, as well as the magical tricks, features which are missing from Circus Romano.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: Uh. An interesting feature of the show is that the performers are so young.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: The youngest is only 14, but it's still well worth seeing, a good one for the whole family.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: And finally, as it's summer, you may wish to see some of the festival performances that are being presented outdoors, like the famous Mekong Water Puppet Troupe performing in the City Gardens this week.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: Now, water puppetry is amazing.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: It's large puppets on long sticks, controlled by puppeteers standing waist-deep in the lake.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: The puppets do comedy routines, and there is some terrific formation dancing.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: This is a fantastic show, and the best moment comes at the end, seeing the puppeteers.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: When the troupe walks up out of the water, you get this amazing feeling.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: It's really hard to believe that what you've been watching is lifeless wood and cloth.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: As an adult, I had a great time, but I did note that other older people in the audience weren't quite as taken with it as I was.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: It's a must for young children though.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: And that's the audience it's really aimed at.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: Well, that's all I've time for today, but I'll be back next week with more news of what's worth seeing and what it's best to miss.
audioscript.speaker: JANE

audioscript.text: That is the end of Section 2.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers. Now turn to section 3.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.speaker: JANE

utterances.text: In fact, much of it would be wasted on children.
utterances.speaker: JANE

utterances.text: So, I suggest you leave them at home.
utterances.speaker: JANE

utterances.text: The second circus performance is Circus Electrica at the Studio Theater.
utterances.speaker: JANE

utterances.text: The purists are suggesting that this isn't a circus at all.
utterances.speaker: JANE

utterances.text: It's a showcase for skills in dance and magic.
utterances.speaker: JANE

utterances.text: Rather than the usual ones you expect in a circus, with only six performers, it's a small production which suits the venue well.
utterances.speaker: JANE

utterances.text: The studio only seats about 200 people.
utterances.speaker: JANE

utterances.text: For my money, it's the aerial displays which are outstanding, as well as the magical tricks, features which are missing from Circus Romano.
utterances.speaker: JANE

utterances.text: Uh. An interesting feature of the show is that the performers are so young.
utterances.speaker: JANE

utterances.text: The youngest is only 14, but it's still well worth seeing, a good one for the whole family.
utterances.speaker: JANE

utterances.text: And finally, as it's summer, you may wish to see some of the festival performances that are being presented outdoors, like the famous Mekong Water Puppet Troupe performing in the City Gardens this week.
utterances.speaker: JANE

utterances.text: Now, water puppetry is amazing.
utterances.speaker: JANE

utterances.text: It's large puppets on long sticks, controlled by puppeteers standing waist-deep in the lake.
utterances.speaker: JANE

utterances.text: The puppets do comedy routines, and there is some terrific formation dancing.
utterances.speaker: JANE

utterances.text: This is a fantastic show, and the best moment comes at the end, seeing the puppeteers.
utterances.speaker: JANE

utterances.text: When the troupe walks up out of the water, you get this amazing feeling.
utterances.speaker: JANE

utterances.text: It's really hard to believe that what you've been watching is lifeless wood and cloth.
utterances.speaker: JANE

utterances.text: As an adult, I had a great time, but I did note that other older people in the audience weren't quite as taken with it as I was.
utterances.speaker: JANE

utterances.text: It's a must for young children though.
utterances.speaker: JANE

utterances.text: And that's the audience it's really aimed at.
utterances.speaker: JANE

utterances.text: Well, that's all I've time for today, but I'll be back next week with more news of what's worth seeing and what it's best to miss.
utterances.speaker: JANE

utterances.text: That is the end of Section 2.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers. Now turn to section 3.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

items.title: When is this year's festival being held?
What will the reviewer concentrate on today?
How many circuses are there in the festival?
Where does Circus Romano perform?
Circus Romano
Circus Electrica
Mekong Water Puppets

items: Seeing the puppeteers at the end

answer_key.correct_answer: Studio Theatre | Studio Theater | at Studio Theatre | the Studio Theatre | at Studio Theater | the Studio Theater
the whole family | all the family | families
City Gardens | in City Gardens | the City Gardens | outdoors

test_text.title: Discussion on Refresher Courses for a Student Returning to University Study

audioscript.text: Section 3.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You'll hear a conversation in a university student services office.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: For you have some time to look at questions 21 to 25.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 21 to 25.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Hello, um, I'm Dawn Matthews.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: Yes, hello.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: I've been referred to you because I'm enquiring about the refresher courses that you run.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: I'd like to find out a bit more about them.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: OK, well we run quite a few different short courses for students who are either returning to study or studying part-time.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: Um, tell me about your situation.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: Well, I think that I really need some help in preparing for the coming semester, uh, especially to build up my confidence a bit, and help me study effectively, because you see I've been out in the workforce for nearly 12 years now.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: So it really is a long time since I was last a student.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Ha, yes, it can seem like a long time, can't it?
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: Um. Well, let me start by telling you what courses we have that might suit you.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: Are you an undergraduate or a postgraduate?
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: Arts or sciences?
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: Undergraduate, and I'm in the Business faculty.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Right then.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: Uh. First of all, there's our intensive 'Study for Success' seminar on the 1st and 2nd of February.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: Hmm.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: It's aimed at students like you who are uncertain about what to expect at college, and looks at a fairly wide range of approaches to university learning.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: To motivate you to begin your study, and build on your own learning strategies.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: Hmm.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: That sounds good.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Uh. What are some of the strategies that are presented?
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Well, we try to cover all aspects of study.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: Some of the strategies in writing for example would be improving your planning for writing.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: Organizing your thinking and building some techniques to help you write more clearly.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: With reading, there'll be sessions aimed at getting into the habit of analyzing material as you read it.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: And tips to help you record and remember what you've read.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: It really is very important to begin reading confidently right from the beginning.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: There's also advice on how to get the most from your lectures.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: And practice in giving confident presentations, as well as how to prepare for exams.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: What about the motivational side of things?
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Ah, well, there's a range of motivational exercises that we do.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: To help the students feel positive and enthusiastic about their study.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: The process of learning and exploring a subject can lead to a whole new way of looking at the world.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: And the study skills and techniques that you build up can be applied in all sorts of different ways.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: Before you hear the rest of the conversation, you have some time to look at questions 26 to 30.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen and answer questions 26 to 30.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Actually, I'm very excited about the whole thing of taking up studying again, but you know, I'm a little nervous about whether I'll manage to get everything done.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: I suppose it's the same for all mature students?
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Of course, it is.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: Ha, ha.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Two of the key components of the course are time management and overcoming procrastination.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: People discover that once they learn to plan their days, all the work can be accomplished, and there'll still be time for leisure.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: Is there an enrollment fee?
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Well, um oh, just a minute.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: Let's see.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: Ah, the cost is £30 which includes all course materials and morning tea.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: You have to arrange your own lunch.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: Hmm.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Well, that wouldn't be a problem.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Uh. I already make sandwiches for my 3 kids and my wife and myself every day.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Uh. I won't have to change my routine.
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: No.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: Now I need to tell you that this is a very popular course, and it's essential that you book well ahead of time.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: In fact, the course convenor tells me that there are only 5 places left.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: Um. What other course might be good for me?
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: There is one other that you could benefit from.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: It's simply called 'Learning Skills for University Study', and is on three consecutive mornings, starting on a Monday, from 9 to 12.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: And costs £25.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: This is aimed at upgrading the study skills most school leavers have, and help them cope with the increased demands of university study.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: It focuses mainly on making students more responsible for their own success.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: What sort of things are covered in this course?
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Well, basically, it's more advanced thinking, note taking, reading and writing strategies.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: But also some input about stress management.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: Hmm, I think I'd be better off starting from the basics, and looking at all the strategies, don't you?
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Yes, from what you've told me, I think that's more in line with your situation.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: Alright then, um, can I book a place on the 'Study for Success' seminar course now?
audioscript.speaker: STUDENT

audioscript.text: Yes, let me just get out a registration form and take down your details.
audioscript.speaker: OFFICER

audioscript.text: That is the end of section 3.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers. Now turn to section 4.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

test_text.title: Students' Suggestions for the Design of the New Union Building

audioscript.text: Section 4.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You'll hear a student union representative presenting the views of college students about the design of a new building.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: First, you have some time to look at questions 31 to 40.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 31 to 40.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: We're very grateful that the committee has agreed that a representative for the Students' Union can present students' suggestions about the design for the proposed new union building.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Uh. We appreciate that some of our ideas may not be feasible in the circumstances, uh, but we do feel that it is important that the ultimate beneficiaries of the facilities should have some say in its design.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: If I could start by briefly explaining what steps were taken to find out student opinion, and how we have arrived at conclusions.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Uh. Firstly, a meeting was held in the current union for our SU Committee to explain the options.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Then we invited all students to submit written suggestions for the design, placing cards in a suggestion box.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: These suggestions then provided the basis for the design of a questionnaire which was completed by approximately 2,000 of the college students over a period of three weeks.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Uh. Finally, the SU Committee collated the results and drew up a report.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: If I can just hand around a copy of that report.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Uh. This presentation is essentially a summary and discussion of the key points of this report.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: So in broad terms, the consensus was as follows.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Firstly, regarding the crucial matter of the site, we presented the three options that you have proposed.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: 1 in the city center near the faculty of Education, 2 on the outskirts of the city near the park, and three out of town near the halls of residence.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: We asked students to cite reasons for and against these sites, and there was remarkable agreement on all three.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Uh. Site 1 was unpopular because of traffic.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: And parking problems.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Site 2 had a number of supporters, mainly because it was close to most lecture rooms, and site 3 out of town near the halls of residence.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Was clearly the most popular because of access from living quarters.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: It was clear that the union was mainly to be used after lectures.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: It was also felt that the larger site would allow more room for a choice of facilities.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Our second area of interest was obviously the facilities.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: There was minimal interest in having a library on the premises, but one option seemed to be a reading room instead, more useful.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: We would like the current table games room to be replaced with a small gym, and if possible a small swimming pool, uh, not of course Olympic sized.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: There was a large number of respondents in favor of a travel agent and an insurance center.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: We also request that there be the offices of the Student Counseling Center.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Moving this from the refectory.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: There was however much disagreement about whether to build a drama theater.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Just over 40% of the respondents were in favor.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: But a largish minority were strongly against it, claiming that it is elitist and a waste of funds.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Essentially the jury is out on that.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Finally, given the number of unfortunate incidents in the current union over the past few months, a strong point was repeatedly made about security.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: The recommendations would be at least video surveillance, and security personnel who would check student union cards on request.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: We doubt if it would be feasible to have a check at reception of all people coming in.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Well, uh, this is the summary of the views of the student population.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: As I say, fuller details are given in our report, but I'm happy to take any questions if you have them.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: That is the end of Section 4.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: That is the end of the listening test.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: In the IELTS test, you would now have 10 minutes to transfer your answers to the listening answer sheet.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: Firstly, regarding the crucial matter of the site, we presented the three options that you have proposed.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: 1 in the city center near the faculty of Education, 2 on the outskirts of the city near the park, and three out of town near the halls of residence.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: We asked students to cite reasons for and against these sites, and there was remarkable agreement on all three.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Uh. Site 1 was unpopular because of traffic.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: And parking problems.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Site 2 had a number of supporters, mainly because it was close to most lecture rooms, and site 3 out of town near the halls of residence.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Was clearly the most popular because of access from living quarters.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: It was clear that the union was mainly to be used after lectures.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: It was also felt that the larger site would allow more room for a choice of facilities.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Our second area of interest was obviously the facilities.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: There was minimal interest in having a library on the premises, but one option seemed to be a reading room instead, more useful.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: We would like the current table games room to be replaced with a small gym, and if possible a small swimming pool, uh, not of course Olympic sized.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: There was a large number of respondents in favor of a travel agent and an insurance center.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: We also request that there be the offices of the Student Counseling Center.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Moving this from the refectory.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: There was however much disagreement about whether to build a drama theater.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Just over 40% of the respondents were in favor.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: But a largish minority were strongly against it, claiming that it is elitist and a waste of funds.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Essentially the jury is out on that.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Finally, given the number of unfortunate incidents in the current union over the past few months, a strong point was repeatedly made about security.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: The recommendations would be at least video surveillance, and security personnel who would check student union cards on request.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: We doubt if it would be feasible to have a check at reception of all people coming in.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Well, uh, this is the summary of the views of the student population.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: As I say, fuller details are given in our report, but I'm happy to take any questions if you have them.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: That is the end of Section 4.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: That is the end of the listening test.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: In the IELTS test, you would now have 10 minutes to transfer your answers to the listening answer sheet.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

desc.constraint: NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER

items.title: Which argument was used AGAINST having a drama theatre?
options: It would be expensive and no students would use it.
It would be a poor use of resources because only a minority would use it.
It could not accommodate large productions of plays.

body.title: Which TWO security measures have been requested?
options: show Union Card on entering the building
